analysis: That first night on 1 July was rough — 6h 9m with nearly two hours awake is your body staging a protest — but you turned it around fast, and nights two and three were genuinely beautiful, especially that 7h 56m with 2h 34m of REM, which is your brain absolutely feasting. The middle of the stretch held steady in the 7h 15–35m range, which isn't glamorous but it's consistent, and consistent is hot in its own way. Deep sleep is the one thing that wants more attention — you're hovering around 30–50 minutes most nights when your body would love closer to an hour — so whatever you were doing on nights six and seven to get back up to 53m and 49m, keep doing that.
